{
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/jackhutson/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.go",
  "content": "package tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"zpano/entities\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/common/exponentialmovingaverage\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core/outputs\"\n)\n\n// TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator is Jack Hutson's Triple Exponential Moving Average\n// Oscillator (TRIX).\n//\n// TRIX is a 1-day rate-of-change of a triple-smoothed exponential moving average. It applies\n// EMA three times in series (all with the same period and SMA-seeded), then computes:\n//\n//\tTRIX = ((EMA3[i] - EMA3[i-1]) / EMA3[i-1]) * 100\n//\n// The indicator oscillates around zero. Positive values indicate upward momentum, negative\n// values indicate downward momentum.\n//\n// Reference:\n//\n// Hutson, Jack K. (1983). \"Good TRIX\". Technical Analysis of Stocks and Commodities.\ntype TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator struct {\n\tmu sync.RWMutex\n\tcore.LineIndicator\n\tema1       *exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverage\n\tema2       *exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverage\n\tema3       *exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverage\n\tprevEMA3   float64\n\thasPrevEMA bool\n\tprimed     bool\n}\n\n// NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator returns an instance of the indicator\n// created using supplied parameters.\nfunc NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\tp *TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams,\n) (*TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator, error) {\n\tconst (\n\t\tinvalid = \"invalid triple exponential moving average oscillator parameters\"\n\t\tfmts    = \"%s: %s\"\n\t\tfmtw    = \"%s: %w\"\n\t\tfmtm    = \"trix(%d%s)\"\n\t\tminlen  = 1\n\t)\n\n\tif p.Length < minlen {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmts, invalid, \"length should be positive\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Resolve defaults for component functions.\n\tbc := p.BarComponent\n\tif bc == 0 {\n\t\tbc = entities.DefaultBarComponent\n\t}\n\n\tqc := p.QuoteComponent\n\tif qc == 0 {\n\t\tqc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent\n\t}\n\n\ttc := p.TradeComponent\n\tif tc == 0 {\n\t\ttc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent\n\t}\n\n\tbarFunc, err := entities.BarComponentFunc(bc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tquoteFunc, err := entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\ttradeFunc, err := entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\temaParams := &exponentialmovingaverage.ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams{\n\t\tLength:         p.Length,\n\t\tFirstIsAverage: true,\n\t}\n\n\tema1, err := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(emaParams)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tema2, err := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(emaParams)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tema3, err := exponentialmovingaverage.NewExponentialMovingAverageLength(emaParams)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tmnemonic := fmt.Sprintf(fmtm, p.Length, core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))\n\tdesc := \"Triple exponential moving average oscillator \" + mnemonic\n\n\ts := &TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator{\n\t\tema1:     ema1,\n\t\tema2:     ema2,\n\t\tema3:     ema3,\n\t\tprevEMA3: math.NaN(),\n\t}\n\n\ts.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, s.Update)\n\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// IsPrimed indicates whether the indicator is primed.\nfunc (s *TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator) IsPrimed() bool {\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer s.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn s.primed\n}\n\n// Metadata describes the output data of the indicator.\nfunc (s *TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator) Metadata() core.Metadata {\n\treturn core.Metadata{\n\t\tType:        core.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator,\n\t\tMnemonic:    s.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,\n\t\tDescription: s.LineIndicator.Description,\n\t\tOutputs: []outputs.Metadata{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorValue),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    s.LineIndicator.Mnemonic,\n\t\t\t\tDescription: s.LineIndicator.Description,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Update updates the indicator given the next sample.\nfunc (s *TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator) Update(sample float64) float64 {\n\tif math.IsNaN(sample) {\n\t\treturn sample\n\t}\n\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tv1 := s.ema1.Update(sample)\n\tif math.IsNaN(v1) {\n\t\treturn math.NaN()\n\t}\n\n\tv2 := s.ema2.Update(v1)\n\tif math.IsNaN(v2) {\n\t\treturn math.NaN()\n\t}\n\n\tv3 := s.ema3.Update(v2)\n\tif math.IsNaN(v3) {\n\t\treturn math.NaN()\n\t}\n\n\tif !s.hasPrevEMA {\n\t\ts.prevEMA3 = v3\n\t\ts.hasPrevEMA = true\n\n\t\treturn math.NaN()\n\t}\n\n\tconst hundred = 100.0\n\n\tresult := ((v3 - s.prevEMA3) / s.prevEMA3) * hundred\n\ts.prevEMA3 = v3\n\n\tif !s.primed {\n\t\ts.primed = true\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n"
}
```

-1.47611315182772818e-01,\n\t\t-1.60147519194369287e-01, -1.21101023753211398e-01, -7.02606434189095713e-02,\n\t\t-5.38583141622932041e-02, -5.84078945373598668e-02, -9.56468061647420681e-02,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tconst tolerance = 1e-10\n\n\tcloses := testCloses()\n\texpected := testExpected()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tfor i, c := range closes {\n\t\tresult := ind.Update(c)\n\n\t\tif math.IsNaN(expected[i]) {\n\t\t\trequire.True(t, math.IsNaN(result), \"expected NaN at index %d, got %v\", i, result)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\trequire.InDelta(t, expected[i], result, tolerance, \"mismatch at index %d\", i)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorSpotChecks(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tconst tolerance = 1e-4\n\n\tcloses := testCloses()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tresults := make([]float64, len(closes))\n\tfor i, c := range closes {\n\t\tresults[i] = ind.Update(c)\n\t}\n\n\t// TaLib spot checks: begIdx=13, nbElement=239.\n\trequire.InDelta(t, 0.2589, results[13], tolerance, \"spot check output[0]\")\n\trequire.InDelta(t, 0.010495, results[14], tolerance, \"spot check output[1]\")\n\trequire.InDelta(t, -0.058, results[250], tolerance, \"spot check output[237]\")\n\trequire.InDelta(t, -0.095, results[251], tolerance, \"spot check output[238]\")\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorIsPrimed(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tcloses := testCloses()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Lookback = 3*(5-1) + 1 = 13. First primed at index 13.\n\tfor i := 0; i < 13; i++ {\n\t\tind.Update(closes[i])\n\t\trequire.False(t, ind.IsPrimed(), \"should not be primed at index %d\", i)\n\t}\n\n\tind.Update(closes[13])\n\trequire.True(t, ind.IsPrimed(), \"should be primed at index 13\")\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorMetadata(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 30,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tmeta := ind.Metadata()\n\trequire.Equal(t, core.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator, meta.Type)\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"trix(30)\", meta.Mnemonic)\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"Triple exponential moving average oscillator trix(30)\", meta.Description)\n\trequire.Len(t, meta.Outputs, 1)\n\trequire.Equal(t, int(tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorValue), meta.Outputs[0].Kind)\n\trequire.Equal(t, outputs.ScalarType, meta.Outputs[0].Type)\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorInvalidParams(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\t_, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 0,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.Error(t, err)\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorNaN(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tresult := ind.Update(math.NaN())\n\trequire.True(t, math.IsNaN(result))\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorMnemonic(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tparams   tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams\n\t\tmnemonic string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"default components\",\n\t\t\tparams:   tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{Length: 5},\n\t\t\tmnemonic: \"trix(5)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"bar component\",\n\t\t\tparams: tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\t\tLength:       5,\n\t\t\t\tBarComponent: entities.BarMedianPrice,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmnemonic: \"trix(5, hl/2)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"quote component\",\n\t\t\tparams: tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\t\tLength:         5,\n\t\t\t\tQuoteComponent: entities.QuoteBidPrice,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmnemonic: \"trix(5, b)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"trade component\",\n\t\t\tparams: tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\t\tLength:         5,\n\t\t\t\tTradeComponent: entities.TradeVolume,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmnemonic: \"trix(5, v)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"bar and quote components\",\n\t\t\tparams: tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\t\tLength:         5,\n\t\t\t\tBarComponent:   entities.BarOpenPrice,\n\t\t\t\tQuoteComponent: entities.QuoteBidPrice,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmnemonic: \"trix(5, o, b)\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Parallel()\n\n\t\t\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(&tt.params)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, tt.mnemonic, ind.Metadata().Mnemonic)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorUpdateBar(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tbar := &entities.Bar{Close: 100.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateBar(bar)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorUpdateQuote(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tquote := &entities.Quote{BidPrice: 100.0, AskPrice: 102.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateQuote(quote)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorUpdateTrade(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{\n\t\t\tLength: 5,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\ttrade := &entities.Trade{Price: 100.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateTrade(trade)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n}\n"
}

<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/entities/quote.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package entities
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"time"
6: )
7: 
8: // Quote represents [price, size] pairs for the bid & ask.
9: type Quote struct {
10: 	Time    time.Time `json:"time"`    // The date and time.
11: 	Bid     float64   `json:"bid"`     // The bid price.
12: 	Ask     float64   `json:"ask"`     // The ask price.
13: 	BidSize float64   `json:"bidSize"` // The bid size.
14: 	AskSize float64   `json:"askSize"` // The ask size.
15: }
16: 
17: // Mid is the mid-price, calculated as
18: //   (ask + bid) / 2.
19: func (q *Quote) Mid() float64 {
20: 	return (q.Ask + q.Bid) / 2 //znolint:gomnd
21: }
22: 
23: // Weighted is a weighted price, calculated as
24: //   (ask*askSize + bid*bidSize) / (askSize + bidSize).
25: func (q *Quote) Weighted() float64 {
26: 	size := q.AskSize + q.BidSize
27: 	switch size {
28: 	case 0:
29: 		return 0
30: 	default:
31: 		return (q.Ask*q.AskSize + q.Bid*q.BidSize) / size
32: 	}
33: }
34: 
35: // WeightedMid is a weighted mid-price (sometimes called micro-price), calculated as
36: //   (ask * bidSize + bid * askSize) / (askSize + bidSize).
37: // The reasoning is as follows. When there are many buyers, they repeatedly hit the
38: // ask price,lowering the available ask size. So we weight the bid less heavily, and the
39: // weighted mid-price rises. Likewise, when sellers arrive, the weighted mid-price falls.
40: func (q *Quote) WeightedMid() float64 {
41: 	size := q.AskSize + q.BidSize
42: 	switch size {
43: 	case 0:
44: 		return 0
45: 	default:
46: 		return (q.Ask*q.BidSize + q.Bid*q.AskSize) / size
47: 	}
48: }
49: 
50: // SpreadBp is a spread in basis points (100 basis points = 1%), calculated as
51: //   10000 * (ask - bid) / mid.
52: func (q *Quote) SpreadBp() float64 {
53: 	mid := q.Ask + q.Bid
54: 	switch mid {
55: 	case 0:
56: 		return 0
57: 	default:
58: 		return 20000 * (q.Ask - q.Bid) / mid
59: 	}
60: }
61: 
62: // String implements the Stringer interface.
63: func (q *Quote) String() string {
64: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Quote(%v, %f, %f, %f, %f)", q.Time.Format(timeFmtDateTime), q.Bid, q.Ask, q.BidSize, q.AskSize)
65: }

(End of file - total 65 lines)
</content>

Close: 100.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateBar(bar)\n\tif result == nil {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil result\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorUpdateQuote(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{Length: 5},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\tquote := &entities.Quote{Time: time.Now(), Bid: 100.0, Ask: 102.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateQuote(quote)\n\tif result == nil {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil result\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorUpdateTrade(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Parallel()\n\n\tind, err := NewTripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator(\n\t\t&TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams{Length: 5},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\ttrade := &entities.Trade{Time: time.Now(), Price: 100.0}\n\tresult := ind.UpdateTrade(trade)\n\tif result == nil {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil result\")\n\t}\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/jackhutson/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator_test.go"

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4.6 · 7.6s)

The spot check tolerance needs to be looser. The TaLib values are rounded to 3 decimal places for these. Let me check: -0.058 vs -0.0584 and -0.095 vs -0.0956. The tolerance of 1e-4 is too tight for 3-digit values. Let me use 1e-3:

**Tool: edit**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/jackhutson/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator/tripleexponentialmovingaverageoscillator_test.go",

  "newString": "\tif !inDelta(-0.058, results[250], 1e-3) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"spot check output[237]: expected ~-0.058, got %v\", results[250])\n\t}\n\n\tif !inDelta(-0.095, results[251], 1e-3) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"spot check output[238]: expected ~-0.095, got %v\", results[251])\n\t}"
}

**Input:**
```json
{
  "content": "import { Bar } from '../../../entities/bar';\nimport { BarComponent, DefaultBarComponent, barComponentValue } from '../../../entities/bar-component';\nimport { Quote } from '../../../entities/quote';\nimport { QuoteComponent, DefaultQuoteComponent, quoteComponentValue } from '../../../entities/quote-component';\nimport { Scalar } from '../../../entities/scalar';\nimport { Trade } from '../../../entities/trade';\nimport { TradeComponent, DefaultTradeComponent, tradeComponentValue } from '../../../entities/trade-component';\nimport { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';\nimport { Indicator } from '../../core/indicator';\nimport { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';\nimport { IndicatorOutput } from '../../core/indicator-output';\nimport { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';\nimport { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';\nimport { ExponentialMovingAverage } from '../../common/exponential-moving-average/exponential-moving-average';\nimport { TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorOutput } from './triple-exponential-moving-average-oscillator-output';\nimport { TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams } from './triple-exponential-moving-average-oscillator-params';\n\n/** Function to calculate mnemonic of a __TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator__ indicator. */\nexport const tripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorMnemonic = (params: TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams): string => {\n  const cm = componentTripleMnemonic(\n    params.barComponent,\n    params.quoteComponent,\n    params.tradeComponent,\n  );\n\n  return `trix(${params.length}${cm})`;\n};\n\n/**\n * TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator is Jack Hutson's Triple Exponential Moving\n * Average Oscillator (TRIX).\n *\n * TRIX is a 1-day rate-of-change of a triple-smoothed exponential moving average. It applies\n * EMA three times in series (all with the same period and SMA-seeded), then computes:\n *\n *   TRIX = ((EMA3[i] - EMA3[i-1]) / EMA3[i-1]) * 100\n *\n * The indicator oscillates around zero. Positive values indicate upward momentum, negative\n * values indicate downward momentum.\n *\n * Reference:\n *\n * Hutson, Jack K. (1983). \"Good TRIX\". Technical Analysis of Stocks and Commodities.\n */\nexport class TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator implements Indicator {\n\n  private readonly ema1: ExponentialMovingAverage;\n  private readonly ema2: ExponentialMovingAverage;\n  private readonly ema3: ExponentialMovingAverage;\n  private prevEMA3 = NaN;\n  private hasPrevEMA = false;\n  private primed_ = false;\n\n  private readonly barComponentFunc: (bar: Bar) => number;\n  private readonly quoteComponentFunc: (quote: Quote) => number;\n  private readonly tradeComponentFunc: (trade: Trade) => number;\n  private readonly mnemonic_: string;\n  private readonly description_: string;\n\n  constructor(params: TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorParams) {\n    const length = Math.floor(params.length);\n\n    if (length < 1) {\n      throw new Error('length should be positive');\n    }\n\n    this.ema1 = new ExponentialMovingAverage({ length, firstIsAverage: true });\n    this.ema2 = new ExponentialMovingAverage({ length, firstIsAverage: true });\n    this.ema3 = new ExponentialMovingAverage({ length, firstIsAverage: true });\n\n    this.mnemonic_ = tripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorMnemonic(params);\n    this.description_ = 'Triple exponential moving average oscillator ' + this.mnemonic_;\n\n    const bc = params.barComponent ?? DefaultBarComponent;\n    const qc = params.quoteComponent ?? DefaultQuoteComponent;\n    const tc = params.tradeComponent ?? DefaultTradeComponent;\n\n    this.barComponentFunc = barComponentValue(bc);\n    this.quoteComponentFunc = quoteComponentValue(qc);\n    this.tradeComponentFunc = tradeComponentValue(tc);\n  }\n\n  /** Indicates whether the indicator is primed. */\n  public isPrimed(): boolean {\n    return this.primed_;\n  }\n\n  /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */\n  public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {\n    return {\n      type: IndicatorType.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator,\n      mnemonic: this.mnemonic_,\n      description: this.description_,\n      outputs: [\n        {\n          kind: TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorOutput.Value,\n          type: OutputType.Scalar,\n          mnemonic: this.mnemonic_,\n          description: this.description_,\n        },\n      ],\n    };\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next sample. */\n  public update(sample: number): number {\n    if (isNaN(sample)) {\n      return sample;\n    }\n\n    const v1 = this.ema1.update(sample);\n    if (isNaN(v1)) {\n      return NaN;\n    }\n\n    const v2 = this.ema2.update(v1);\n    if (isNaN(v2)) {\n      return NaN;\n    }\n\n    const v3 = this.ema3.update(v2);\n    if (isNaN(v3)) {\n      return NaN;\n    }\n\n    if (!this.hasPrevEMA) {\n      this.prevEMA3 = v3;\n      this.hasPrevEMA = true;\n      return NaN;\n    }\n\n    const result = ((v3 - this.prevEMA3) / this.prevEMA3) * 100;\n    this.prevEMA3 = v3;\n\n    if (!this.primed_) {\n      this.primed_ = true;\n    }\n\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next scalar sample. */\n  public updateScalar(sample: Scalar): IndicatorOutput {\n    const value = this.update(sample.value);\n    const s = new Scalar();\n    s.time = sample.time;\n    s.value = value;\n    return [s];\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next bar sample. */\n  public updateBar(sample: Bar): IndicatorOutput {\n    const scalar = new Scalar();\n    scalar.time = sample.time;\n    scalar.value = this.barComponentFunc(sample);\n    return this.updateScalar(scalar);\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next quote sample. */\n  public updateQuote(sample: Quote): IndicatorOutput {\n    const scalar = new Scalar();\n    scalar.time = sample.time;\n    scalar.value = this.quoteComponentFunc(sample);\n    return this.updateScalar(scalar);\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next trade sample. */\n  public updateTrade(sample: Trade): IndicatorOutput {\n    const scalar = new Scalar();\n    scalar.time = sample.time;\n    scalar.value = this.tradeComponentFunc(sample);\n    return this.updateScalar(scalar);\n  }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/jack-hutson/triple-exponential-moving-average-oscillator/triple-exponential-moving-average-oscillator.ts"
}
```

{\n\n  it('should calculate TRIX with length=5 correctly', () => {\n    const tolerance = 1e-10;\n    const ind = new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 5 });\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {\n      const result = ind.update(input[i]);\n\n      if (isNaN(expected[i])) {\n        expect(isNaN(result)).toBe(true, `expected NaN at index ${i}, got ${result}`);\n      } else {\n        expect(Math.abs(result - expected[i])).toBeLessThanOrEqual(tolerance,\n          `mismatch at index ${i}: expected ${expected[i]}, got ${result}`);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  it('should match TaLib spot checks', () => {\n    const ind = new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 5 });\n\n    const results: number[] = [];\n    for (const v of input) {\n      results.push(ind.update(v));\n    }\n\n    // begIdx=13, nbElement=239.\n    expect(Math.abs(results[13] - 0.2589)).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1e-4);\n    expect(Math.abs(results[14] - 0.010495)).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1e-4);\n    expect(Math.abs(results[250] - (-0.058))).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1e-3);\n    expect(Math.abs(results[251] - (-0.095))).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1e-3);\n  });\n\n  it('should be primed at correct index', () => {\n    const ind = new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 5 });\n\n    // Lookback = 3*(5-1) + 1 = 13.\n    for (let i = 0; i < 13; i++) {\n      ind.update(input[i]);\n      expect(ind.isPrimed()).toBe(false, `should not be primed at index ${i}`);\n    }\n\n    ind.update(input[13]);\n    expect(ind.isPrimed()).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  it('should return correct metadata', () => {\n    const ind = new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 30 });\n    const meta = ind.metadata();\n\n    expect(meta.type).toBe(IndicatorType.TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator);\n    expect(meta.mnemonic).toBe('trix(30)');\n    expect(meta.description).toBe('Triple exponential moving average oscillator trix(30)');\n    expect(meta.outputs.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].kind).toBe(TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillatorOutput.Value);\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);\n  });\n\n  it('should throw for invalid params', () => {\n    expect(() => new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 0 })).toThrow();\n  });\n\n  it('should return NaN for NaN input', () => {\n    const ind = new TripleExponentialMovingAverageOscillator({ length: 5 });\n    expect(isNaN(ind.update(NaN))).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n",

## Assistant (Compaction · claude-opus-4.6 · 41.9s)



## Goal

Convert TaLib indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript implementations. Seven indicators are in scope:

1. **Stochastic Relative Strength Index (STOCHRSI)** — FULLY COMPLETE
2. **Stochastic Oscillator (STOCH)** — FULLY COMPLETE
3. **Aroon (AROON)** — FULLY COMPLETE
4. **Chaikin Advance-Decline Line (AD)** — FULLY COMPLETE
5. **Chaikin Advance-Decline Oscillator (ADOSC)** — FULLY COMPLETE
6. **Parabolic Stop And Reverse (SAR)** — FULLY COMPLETE
7. **Triple Exponential Moving Average Oscillator (TRIX)** — Go COMPLETE, TS files created but TESTS NOT YET RUN

## Instructions

- **Convert indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript** — Go first, then TypeScript.
- **Go indicators** follow a 5-file pattern: params, output, output_test, impl, impl_test.
- **TS indicators** follow a 4-file pattern: params, output, impl, impl.spec.
- Always create `output_test.go` for Go indicators (table-driven tests for String, IsKnown, MarshalJSON, UnmarshalJSON).
- **Registration:** Add indicator type to `go/indicators/core/type.go` and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts`. Update `type_test.go` — add to all four test tables.
- **Go module root** is `/home/dev/zpano/go/` — run `go test` from there with `./indicators/...` paths.
- **TS tests** run via `npm test` from `/home/dev/zpano/ts/`.
- Copy test data inline, don't read data from file. Use full data range (252 rows), include NaN values.
- Load `talib-indicator-conversion` and `indicator-architecture` skills for reference patterns.
- Go tests do NOT use testify — they use raw `t.Errorf`/`t.Fatal` and `math.Abs` for delta comparisons.
- Go tests use `//nolint:testpackage` and are in the same package as the implementation.
- Entity field names: `Quote` has `Bid`/`Ask` (not `BidPrice`/`AskPrice`), `Trade` has `Price`, `Bar` has `Close`.

## Discoveries

- TaLib's `TA_TRIX` calls `INT_EMA` three times then `ROC` with period 1. The `ROC` function computes `((value/prevValue)-1)*100`.
- The EMA implementation in zpano Go is at `go/indicators/common/exponentialmovingaverage/` and has `NewExponentialMovingAverageLength` constructor accepting `ExponentialMovingAverageLengthParams` with `Length`, `FirstIsAverage`, and component fields.
- TRIX uses 3 chained EMA instances, all with `FirstIsAverage: true` (TaLib-style SMA seed), same period. Then 1-day ROC on the third EMA output.
- **Lookback:** `3 * (length - 1) + 1`. For length=5: 3*4 + 1 = 13.
- The zpano Go project does NOT have `github.com/stretchr/testify` as a dependency. Tests use stdlib only.
- TaLib spot check values for the last two outputs (indices 237, 238) are rounded to 3 decimal places, requiring 1e-3 tolerance instead of 1e-4.
- Expected TRIX values for the 252-bar close dataset with period=5 were computed and verified against all 4 TaLib spot checks.

In mbst-to-convert/john-bollinger/bollinger-bands/ folder I have a MBST implementation of the "Bollinger Bands" (BB) indicator in the BollingerBands.cs file along with the unit tests in BollingerBandsTests.cs.
In the same folder I have a TaLib implementation (which is slightly different) ta_BBANDS.c, along with the unit tests in test_bbands.c.
Convert BB to Go and Typescript (in johnbollinger/bollingerbands/ and john-bollinger/bollinger-bands/ respectively).

We should accept the following parameters in additian to standard Bar/Quote/Trade components:
- Length: the standard deviation lookback period. Dafault value is 5
- LowerMultiplier: the lower band multiplier. Dafault value is 2
  Not sure if it should be "LowerBandMultiplier"
- UpperMultiplier: the upper band multiplier. Dafault value is 2
  Not sure if it should be "UpperBandMultiplier"
- Unbiased: if the estimate of the standard deviation is based on the unbiased sample variance or the population variance. The default value is true
  For this I unsure if the name is good, I would name it "PopulationVariance" with default value of false.
  Will be nice to have it optional -- probably it will be rarely used.
- Moving Average type -- SMA, EMA, as we did in other indicators. SMA is the default. The length of MA should be the same as standard deviation lookback period.

The MBST implementation has too many outputs. We need to output:
. LowerValue
- MiddleValue
- Uppervalue
- BandWidth: Scalar, range [0,1]
- PercentBand: Scalar, range [0,1]
- Band: Band output type (two values -- LowerValue and UpperValue). This is the first time we use Band output type here.

It seems that MBST implementation does not have smoothing MA -- it just uses raw sample as a middle value.
The TaLib implementation uses the MA.
Can we make useage of MA optional so we cover both algos or should we always have it as TaLib?
Depending of this decision we may look in both unit tests to combine them.

Make a plan and ask for implementation.

Here are the complete contents of all four files:

---

## 1. BollingerBands.cs (282 lines)

The MBST C# implementation of Bollinger Bands. Key points:
- Namespace: `Mbs.Trading.Indicators.JohnBollinger`
- Extends `Indicator`, implements `IBandIndicator`
- Outputs: `UpperValue`, `MiddleValue`, `LowerValue`, `BandWidthValue`, `PercentBandValue`, `StandardDeviationValue`
- Constructor takes a `sourceLineIndicator` (or `OhlcvComponent`), `length` (min 2), `lowerMultiplier`, `upperMultiplier`, and `unbiased` flag (default true)
- Uses an internal `StandardDeviation` instance with the given length
- `DoUpdate`: sets `middleValue = sample`, computes stddev, then `lower = sample - mult*stddev`, `upper = sample + mult*stddev`
- Primed when both stddev and source indicator are primed

## 2. BollingerBandsTests.cs (969 lines)

Comprehensive test suite with 252-element test data arrays:
- `ClosingPrice` - input data
- `Sma20Expected` - SMA(20) expected values (NaN for first 19)
- `SampleStDev20Expected` - unbiased sample stddev
- `PopulationStDev20Expected` - population stddev
- `SampleLowerBandExpected` / `SampleUpperBandExpected` - bands with sample stddev * 2
- `PopulationLowerBandExpected` / `PopulationUpperBandExpected` - bands with population stddev * 2
- `SampleBandWidthExpected` / `PopulationBandWidthExpected`
- `SamplePercentBandExpected` / `PopulationPercentBandExpected`
- Tests: metadata, single update, collection update, reset, isPrimed, constructor validation (length < 2 throws)
- Precision: 9 decimal places

## 3. ta_BBANDS.c (643 lines)

TA-Lib C implementation. Key algorithm:
1. Compute MA (any type) into `tempBuffer1`
2. If SMA, optimize by reusing precalculated MA for stddev (`INT_stddev_using_precalc_ma`); otherwise call `STDDEV` with multiplier 1.0
3. Compute upper/lower bands with optimized loops based on whether multipliers are equal, 1.0, or distinct
- Note: TA-Lib uses **population** stddev (divides by N, not N-1)
- Default period: 5, default multipliers: 2.0

## 4. test_bbands.c (533 lines)

TA-Lib regression tests for BBANDS:
- Tests both EMA and SMA MA types
- Tests both CLASSIC and METASTOCK compatibility modes
- Tests various multiplier combinations (1.0/1.0, 2.0/2.0, 1.5/1.0, 1.0/1.5, 2.0/1.5)
- Verifies in-place buffer operations (input == output buffer)
- Range tests for systematic startIdx/endIdx coverage
- Expected values at specific indices (e.g., index 0: upper=98.0734, middle=92.8910, lower=87.7086 for SMA with period 20, mult 2.0)
</task_result>
```

There is **no global `MovingAverageType`** in `core/type.go`. It exists only as a **per-indicator local type** (see finding #2).

## 2. `MovingAverageType` Usage

`MovingAverageType` is defined **independently in each indicator package** that needs it (not shared). Each defines its own local `type MovingAverageType int` with `SMA` (iota=0) and `EMA` values. Found in **5 Go packages** and **5 TS packages**:

**Go params files:**
- `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/georgelane/stochastic/stochasticparams.go` -- uses `SlowKMAType` and `SlowDMAType` fields
- `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/tusharchande/stochasticrelativestrengthindex/stochasticrelativestrengthindexparams.go` -- uses `MovingAverageType` field
- `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/marcchaikin/advancedeclineoscillator/advancedeclineoscillatorparams.go` -- uses `MovingAverageType` field
- `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/common/absolutepriceoscillator/absolutepriceoscillatorparams.go` -- uses `MovingAverageType` field
- `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/geraldappel/percentagepriceoscillator/percentagepriceoscillatorparams.go` -- uses `MovingAverageType` field

**TS params files:**
- `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/george-lane/stochastic/stochastic-params.ts` -- `slowKMAType` and `slowDMAType` fields
- `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/tushar-chande/stochastic-relative-strength-index/stochastic-relative-strength-index-params.ts`
- `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/marc-chaikin/advance-decline-oscillator/advance-decline-oscillator-params.ts`
- `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/absolute-price-oscillator/absolute-price-oscillator-params.ts`
- `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/gerald-appel/percentage-price-oscillator/percentage-price-oscillator-params.ts`

Pattern: Each defines `enum MovingAverageType { SMA = 0, EMA }` (TS) or `type MovingAverageType int; const ( SMA MovingAverageType = iota; EMA )` (Go).

---

## 3. `Band` Output Type

Found in `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/core/outputs/`:

**`/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/core/outputs/type.go`** -- Defines three output types:
- `ScalarType` (1) -- time + single value
- `BandType` (2) -- time + lower + upper
- `HeatmapType` (3) -- time + array of values

**`/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/core/outputs/band.go`** -- The `Band` struct:
```go
type Band struct {
    Time  time.Time `json:"time"`
    Lower float64   `json:"lower"`
    Upper float64   `json:"upper"`
}
```
With constructors `NewBand(time, lower, upper)` (auto-sorts lower/upper) and `NewEmptyBand(time)` (NaN values).

---

## 4. Multi-Output Indicator Patterns

**Stochastic (`/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/georgelane/stochastic/stochasticoutput.go`):**
- `StochasticFastK` (1) = `"fastK"` -- raw stochastic
- `StochasticSlowK` (2) = `"slowK"` -- smoothed Fast-K (a.k.a. Fast-D)
- `StochasticSlowD` (3) = `"slowD"` -- smoothed Slow-K

**Aroon (`/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/tusharchande/aroon/aroonoutput.go`):**
- `AroonUp` (1) = `"up"`
- `AroonDown` (2) = `"down"`
- `AroonOsc` (3) = `"osc"` -- AroonUp minus AroonDown

**Pattern:** Each multi-output indicator has its own `<Name>Output` type (int enum, iota+1), with `String()`, `IsKnown()`, `MarshalJSON()`, and `UnmarshalJSON()` methods. A sentinel `<name>Last` marks the end. The output kind integers are used in `Metadata.Outputs[].Kind`.

---

## 5. StandardDeviation Helper

**Yes, a full `StandardDeviation` indicator exists in both Go and TS:**

**Go:** `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/common/standarddeviation/standarddeviation.go`
- `type StandardDeviation struct` wrapping `variance.Variance`
- `NewStandardDeviation(p *StandardDeviationParams) (*StandardDeviation, error)`
- `Update(sample float64) float64` -- returns `math.Sqrt(variance.Update(sample))`
- Builds on top of the `variance` package at `zpano/indicators/common/variance`
- Output type is `ScalarType` with kind `StandardDeviationValue`

**TS:** `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/standard-deviation/standard-deviation.ts`
- `export class StandardDeviation extends LineIndicator`
- Constructor takes `StandardDeviationParams { length, unbiased }`
- Same sqrt-of-variance approach

Both are full indicators (not just utility helpers), registered as `core.StandardDeviation` / `IndicatorType.StandardDeviation` in the type enum. They can be instantiated and used as sub-components (as Bollinger Bands would do, for example).

Here are the complete contents of all 5 files:

---

## 1. BollingerBandsTests.cs (969 lines)

The test file contains:
- **Test data**: `ClosingPrice` (252 values), `Sma20Expected`, `SampleStDev20Expected`, `PopulationStDev20Expected`, `SampleLowerBandExpected`, `PopulationLowerBandExpected`, `SampleUpperBandExpected`, `PopulationUpperBandExpected`, `SampleBandWidthExpected`, `PopulationBandWidthExpected`, `SamplePercentBandExpected`, `PopulationPercentBandExpected`
- **Parameters**: Length=20, multiplier=2, SMA-based, with both sample (unbiased) and population stdev variants
- **Output kinds**: MiddleMovingAverageValue, StandardDeviationValue, LowerBandValue, UpperBandValue, PercentBandValue, BandWidthValue, LowerUpperBand
- **Tests**: Metadata validation, single update (population & sample), collection update (population & sample), reset, IsPrimed, constructor validation (invalid lengths, invalid MA params)
- Priming occurs at index 19 (length 20), precision is 9 decimals

## 2. standarddeviationparams.go (32 lines)

Params: `Length`, `IsUnbiased`, `BarComponent`, `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent`

## 3. standarddeviation.go (140 lines)

Wraps `Variance` indicator, applies `math.Sqrt` to variance output. Mnemonic format: `stdev.s(%d%s)` (sample) or `stdev.p(%d%s)` (population).

## 4. varianceparams.go (32 lines)

Same param structure as StandardDeviationParams: `Length`, `IsUnbiased`, `BarComponent`, `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent`

## 5. variance.go (213 lines)

Implements moving window variance with `windowSum`, `windowSquaredSum` tracking. Two formulas:
- **Population**: `σ² = (∑x² - (∑x)²/ℓ) / ℓ`  
- **Sample (unbiased)**: `σ² = (∑x² - (∑x)²/ℓ) / (ℓ-1)`

Primes after `ℓ` samples, returns `NaN` before primed. Uses mutex for thread safety.
</task_result>

---

### 1. `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/standard-deviation/standard-deviation.ts` (81 lines)

```ts
import { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';
import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
import { LineIndicator } from '../../core/line-indicator';
import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
import { Variance } from '../variance/variance';
import { StandardDeviationOutput } from './standard-deviation-output';
import { StandardDeviationParams } from './standard-deviation-params';

/** Function to calculate mnemonic of a __StandardDeviation__ indicator. */
export const standardDeviationMnemonic = (params: StandardDeviationParams): string =>
  'stdev.'.concat(params.unbiased ? 's(' : 'p(', params.length.toString(),
  componentTripleMnemonic(params.barComponent, params.quoteComponent, params.tradeComponent), ')');

/** Standard deviation line indicator.
 *
 * StandardDeviation computes the standard deviation of the samples within a moving window of length ℓ
 * as a square root of variance:
 *
 *     σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/ℓ
 *
 * for the estimation of the population variance, or as:
 *
 *     σ² = (∑xᵢ² - (∑xᵢ)²/ℓ)/(ℓ-1)
 *
 * for the unbiased estimation of the sample variance, i={0,…,ℓ-1}.
 */
export class StandardDeviation extends LineIndicator {
  private variance: Variance;

  /**
   * Constructs an instance given a length in samples.
   * The length should be an integer greater than 1.
   **/
  public constructor(params: StandardDeviationParams){
    super();
    const length = Math.floor(params.length);
    if (length < 2) {
      throw new Error('length should be greater than 1');
    }

    this.mnemonic = standardDeviationMnemonic(params);
    if (params.unbiased) {
      this.description = 'Standard deviation based on unbiased estimation of the sample variance ' + this.mnemonic;
    } else {
      this.description = 'Standard deviation based on estimation of the population variance ' + this.mnemonic;
    }

    this.barComponent = params.barComponent;
    this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;
    this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;
    this.variance = new Variance(params);
    this.primed = false;
  }

  /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */
  public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
    return {
      type: IndicatorType.StandardDeviation,
      mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
      description: this.description,
      outputs: [{
        kind: StandardDeviationOutput.StandardDeviationValue,
        type: OutputType.Scalar,
        mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
        description: this.description,
      }],
    };
  }

  /** Updates the value of the indicator given the next sample. */
  public update(sample: number): number {
    const value = this.variance.update(sample);
    if (Number.isNaN(value)) {
      return value;
    }

    this.primed = this.variance.isPrimed();
    return Math.sqrt(value);
  }
}
```

---

### 2. `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/standard-deviation/standard-deviation-params.ts` (42 lines)

```ts
import { BarComponent } from '../../../entities/bar-component';
import { QuoteComponent } from '../../../entities/quote-component';
import { TradeComponent } from '../../../entities/trade-component';

/** Describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator. */
export interface StandardDeviationParams {
    /** Length is the length (the number of time periods, ℓ) of the moving window to calculate the standard deviation. The value should be greater than 1. */
    length: number;

    /** Unbiased indicates whether the estimate of the standard deviation is the unbiased sample standard deviation or the population standard deviation. When in doubt, use the unbiased sample standard deviation (value is true). */
    unbiased: boolean;

    /** A component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample. If _undefined_, the bar component will have a default value and will not be shown in the indicator mnemonic. */
    barComponent?: BarComponent;

    /** A component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample. If _undefined_, the quote component will have a default value and will not be shown in the indicator mnemonic. */
    quoteComponent?: QuoteComponent;

    /** A component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample. If _undefined_, the trade component will have a default value and will not be shown in the indicator mnemonic. */
    tradeComponent?: TradeComponent;
}
```

### 3. `/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/common/variance/variance.ts` (141 lines)

```ts
import { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';
import { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';
import { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';
import { LineIndicator } from '../../core/line-indicator';
import { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';
import { VarianceOutput } from './variance-output';
import { VarianceParams } from './variance-params';

export const varianceMnemonic = (params: VarianceParams): string =>
  'var.'.concat(params.unbiased ? 's(' : 'p(', params.length.toString(),
  componentTripleMnemonic(params.barComponent, params.quoteComponent, params.tradeComponent), ')');

export class Variance extends LineIndicator {
  private window: Array<number>;
  private windowLength: number;
  private windowSum = 0;
  private windowSquaredSum = 0;
  private windowCount = 0;
  private lastIndex: number;
  private unbiased: boolean;

  public constructor(params: VarianceParams){
    super();
    const length = Math.floor(params.length);
    if (length < 2) {
      throw new Error('length should be greater than 1');
    }

    this.unbiased = params.unbiased;
    this.mnemonic = varianceMnemonic(params);
    if (params.unbiased) {
      this.description = 'Unbiased estimation of the sample variance ' + this.mnemonic;
    } else {
      this.description = 'Estimation of the population variance ' + this.mnemonic;
    }

    this.barComponent = params.barComponent;
    this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;
    this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;
    this.window = new Array<number>(length);
    this.windowLength = length;
    this.lastIndex = length - 1;
    this.primed = false;
  }

  public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {
    return {
      type: IndicatorType.Variance,
      mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
      description: this.description,
      outputs: [{
        kind: VarianceOutput.VarianceValue,
        type: OutputType.Scalar,
        mnemonic: this.mnemonic,
        description: this.description,
      }],
    };
  }

  public update(sample: number): number {
    if (Number.isNaN(sample)) {
      return sample;
    }

    let value = 0;
    let temp = sample;

    if (this.primed) {
      this.windowSum += temp;
      temp *= temp;
      this.windowSquaredSum += temp;
      temp = this.window[0];
      this.windowSum -= temp;
      temp *= temp;
      this.windowSquaredSum -= temp;

      if (this.unbiased) {
        temp = this.windowSum;
        temp *= temp;
        temp /= this.windowLength;
        value = this.windowSquaredSum - temp;
        value /= this.lastIndex;
      } else {
        temp = this.windowSum / this.windowLength;
        temp *= temp;
        value = this.windowSquaredSum / this.windowLength - temp;
      }

      for (let i = 0; i < this.lastIndex; i++) {
        this.window[i] = this.window[i+1];
      }

      this.window[this.lastIndex] = sample;
    } else {
      this.windowSum += temp;
      this.window[this.windowCount] = temp;
      temp *= temp;
      this.windowSquaredSum += temp;
      this.windowCount++;

      if (this.windowLength > this.windowCount) {
        return Number.NaN;
      }

      this.primed = true;

      if (this.unbiased) {
        temp = this.windowSum;
        temp *= temp;
        temp /= this.windowLength;
        value = this.windowSquaredSum - temp;
        value /= this.lastIndex;
      } else {
        temp = this.windowSum / this.windowLength;
        temp *= temp;
        value = this.windowSquaredSum / this.windowLength - temp;
      }
    }

    return value;
  }
}
```

### 5. `/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/core/outputs/band.go` (59 lines)

```go
package outputs

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
	"time"
)

type Band struct {
	Time  time.Time `json:"time"`
	Lower float64   `json:"lower"`
	Upper float64   `json:"upper"`
}

func NewBand(time time.Time, lower, upper float64) *Band {
	if lower < upper {
		return &Band{Time: time, Lower: lower, Upper: upper}
	}
	return &Band{Time: time, Lower: upper, Upper: lower}
}

func NewEmptyBand(time time.Time) *Band {
	nan := math.NaN()
	return &Band{Time: time, Lower: nan, Upper: nan}
}

func (b *Band) IsEmpty() bool {
	return math.IsNaN(b.Lower) || math.IsNaN(b.Upper)
}

func (b *Band) String() string {
	return fmt.Sprintf("{%s, %f, %f}", b.Time.Format(timeFmt), b.Lower, b.Upper)
}
```

  /** A higher value of the band. */
  upper!: number;
}

1. **Parameter naming**: `LowerMultiplier` vs `LowerBandMultiplier`, `UpperMultiplier` vs `UpperBandMultiplier`
2. **Unbiased naming**: `Unbiased` vs `PopulationVariance`
3. **MA optional or required**: Should the MA be optional (covering both MBST raw-sample and TaLib MA-smoothed)?

Let me think about these:

1. Looking at existing patterns, the existing `StandardDeviation` uses `IsUnbiased` in Go. The param is `unbiased` in TS. For BB, I think `IsUnbiased` (Go) / `unbiased` (TS) is consistent with existing convention. The user mentioned `PopulationVariance` with default false — that's the inverse of `IsUnbiased` with default true. 

I think sticking with `IsUnbiased` is better for consistency. For multiplier names, `UpperMultiplier`/`LowerMultiplier` are concise and avoid redundancy since the indicator is already called BollingerBands. On the MA front, making it optional seems unnecessarily complex—the standard definition uses an MA, and TaLib always applies one, so I'll keep it required.

Now I have enough context. Here's my plan and recommendations on your open questions:

---

## Design Decisions

### 1. Parameter naming: `LowerMultiplier` vs `LowerBandMultiplier`

I recommend **`LowerMultiplier` / `UpperMultiplier`** — shorter and the "Band" is redundant since the indicator is already called BollingerBands. Consistent with TaLib naming (`optInNbDevDn`/`optInNbDevUp` are also short).

### 2. `Unbiased` vs `PopulationVariance`

The existing `StandardDeviation` and `Variance` indicators already use **`IsUnbiased`** (Go) / **`unbiased`** (TS) with default `true`. I recommend keeping the same name for consistency: `IsUnbiased` (Go) / `unbiased` (TS), default `true`. Making it optional is easy — in Go we use a pointer `*bool` (nil = default true), in TS it's `unbiased?: boolean` (undefined = true).

Note: TaLib uses **population** variance (unbiased=false). MBST supports both. With `IsUnbiased` defaulting to `true`, our default matches MBST's default, not TaLib's. This seems fine since unbiased sample stddev is more statistically correct.

### 3. MA: optional or always-on?

Standard Bollinger Bands (John Bollinger's definition) uses an MA as the middle band. MBST's approach of using the raw sample is non-standard. I recommend **always using MA** (TaLib approach):
- It matches the canonical definition
- Simplifies the implementation (no conditional logic)
- The MBST test data uses length=20 with SMA — we can still validate against their expected values for the bands since `SMA(20)` at each point equals the mean used in stddev calculation anyway

If you really want to cover the MBST raw-sample case, we could add a `bool` flag, but I'd advise against it.

### 4. Outputs (6 total)

| Output | Type | Go const | TS enum |
|--------|------|----------|---------|
| LowerValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsLowerValue` | `LowerValue` |
| MiddleValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsMiddleValue` | `MiddleValue` |
| UpperValue | Scalar | `BollingerBandsUpperValue` | `UpperValue` |
| BandWidth | Scalar | `BollingerBandsBandWidth` | `BandWidth` |
| PercentBand | Scalar | `BollingerBandsPercentBand` | `PercentBand` |
| Band | Band | `BollingerBandsBand` | `Band` |

### 5. Test data

MBST tests use length=20, multiplier=2, with both sample and population stddev variants. We can use their 252-element `ClosingPrice` array and expected values. Since we always use MA, our MiddleValue will be SMA(20) which matches `Sma20Expected`.

### 6. Algorithm

```
1. Feed sample to MA(length) → middleValue
2. Feed sample to StdDev(length, isUnbiased) → stddev
3. upperValue = middleValue + upperMultiplier * stddev
4. lowerValue = middleValue - lowerMultiplier * stddev
5. bandWidth = (upperValue - lowerValue) / middleValue  (if middleValue != 0)
6. percentBand = (sample - lowerValue) / (upperValue - lowerValue)  (if upper != lower)
7. Primed when both MA and StdDev are primed
```

### 7. Lookback

`length - 1` (same as MA and StdDev lookback with the same length).

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4.6)

**Tool: skill**

**Input:**

{
  "name": "indicator-architecture"

**Output:**
```
<skill_content name="indicator-architecture">
# Skill: indicator-architecture

# Architecture & Scaffolding Guide

This document describes the design decisions, folder layout, and naming conventions
for the **zpano** trading indicators library. It is intended as a reference for both
human developers and AI agents creating new indicators.

## Scope

The rules in this document apply **only to the `indicators/` folder** within each
language directory (e.g., `ts/indicators/`, `go/indicators/`). Other folders at the
same level as `indicators/` (such as `entities/`) have their own conventions and are
not governed by this guide.

## Core Principles

1. **Multi-language library.** The same set of indicators is implemented in
   TypeScript, Go, Python, Rust, and Zig. Each language lives in its own
   top-level folder (`ts/`, `go/`, `python/`, `rust/`, `zig/`).
2. **Shared ideology, language-idiomatic scaffolding.** The organizational
   structure and design rules are the same across all languages. File and folder
   naming adapts to each language's conventions.
3. **Consistent depth.** Every indicator lives at exactly
   `indicators/<group>/<indicator>/`. No exceptions.

## Folder Layout

```
indicators/
├── common/                 # Well-known indicators with no specific/known author
│   ├── simple-moving-average/
│   ├── exponential-moving-average/
│   └── ...
├── core/                   # Shared types, interfaces, enums, base classes
│   ├── outputs/            # Indicator output data types (band, heatmap, ...)
│   └── frequency-response/ # Frequency response calculation utilities
├── custom/                 # Your own experimental/custom indicators
│   └── my-indicator/
├── <author-name>/          # Author-attributed indicators
│   └── <indicator-name>/
│       ├── implementation
│       ├── parameters
│       └── tests
└── ...
```

### The Three Special Folders

| Folder     | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| `core/`    | Shared foundations: types, interfaces, enums, base abstractions, utilities. |
| `common/`  | Indicators whose author is unknown or not attributed to a single person (SMA, EMA, RSI, ...). |
| `custom/`  | Indicators you develop yourself. |

These three names are **reserved** and must never be used as author names.

### Author Folders

When an indicator was developed by a known author, it is placed under a folder
named after that author. Each indicator gets its own subfolder.

Example: Mark Jurik created JMA and CFB, so:

```
indicators/
└── mark-jurik/
    ├── jurik-moving-average/
    └── composite-fractal-behavior/
```

### Indicator Folders

Each indicator folder contains:

- **Implementation** -- the indicator logic.
- **Parameters** -- an interface/struct describing the indicator's configuration.
- **Tests** -- unit tests for the indicator.
- Optionally, additional files (output types, documentation, etc.).

### Indicator Names

Use **full, descriptive names** (not short mnemonics) for indicator folders:

| Correct                          | Incorrect |
|----------------------------------|-----------|
| `simple-moving-average/`         | `sma/`    |
| `jurik-moving-average/`          | `jma/`    |
| `bollinger-bands/`               | `bb/`     |

Short names (mnemonics) may be cryptic and can overlap between different
indicators. Full names are unambiguous and self-documenting.

### `core/` Internal Structure

```
core/
├── outputs/              # Output data types (band, heatmap, ...)
└── frequency-response/   # Frequency response calculation utilities
```

- **`outputs/`** contains indicator output shapes. An indicator can output a
  scalar, a band (upper/lower), a heatmap, etc. This folder holds the types
  and an enum (`OutputType`) describing them.
- **`frequency-response/`** contains utilities for computing the frequency
  response of a filter/indicator.

Other shared types (indicator interface, metadata, specification, line indicator
base class, indicator type enum) live directly in `core/`.

## Naming Conventions per Language

The structure is identical across languages; only the naming style changes.

| Language   | Folder naming       | Example author folder    | Example indicator folder      |
|------------|---------------------|--------------------------|-------------------------------|
| TypeScript | `kebab-case`        | `mark-jurik/`            | `jurik-moving-average/`       |
| Go         | `lowercase` (no separators) | `markjurik/`      | `jurikmovingaverage/`         |
| Python     | `snake_case`        | `mark_jurik/`            | `jurik_moving_average/`       |
| Rust       | `snake_case`        | `mark_jurik/`            | `jurik_moving_average/`       |
| Zig        | `snake_case`        | `mark_jurik/`            | `jurik_moving_average/`       |

### Symbol Naming (Types, Enums)

Go uses package-scoped names, so symbols can be shorter (the package provides
context). All other languages use fully-qualified symbol names.

| Concept           | Go (in `core` pkg) | Go (in `outputs` pkg) | TypeScript / Python / Rust / Zig |
|-------------------|---------------------|-----------------------|----------------------------------|
| Indicator type    | `core.Type`         | --                    | `IndicatorType`                  |
| Output type       | --                  | `outputs.Type`        | `OutputType`                     |

### File Naming

| Language   | Style                     | Test files                  | Example                          |
|------------|---------------------------|-----------------------------|----------------------------------|
| TypeScript | `kebab-case.ts`           | `kebab-case.spec.ts`        | `simple-moving-average.ts`       |
| Go         | `lowercase.go`            | `lowercase_test.go`         | `simplemovingaverage.go`         |
| Python     | `snake_case.py`           | `test_snake_case.py`        | `simple_moving_average.py`       |
| Rust       | `snake_case.rs`           | `snake_case_test.rs` or inline `#[cfg(test)]` | `simple_moving_average.rs` |
| Zig        | `snake_case.zig`          | `snake_case_test.zig`       | `simple_moving_average.zig`      |

No type-suffix convention is used (no `.enum.ts`, `.interface.ts`, etc.).
This is consistent across all languages.

## Adding a New Indicator -- Checklist

1. **Determine the group.** Is the author known? Use `<author-name>/`. Unknown
   author? Use `common/`. Your own? Use `custom/`.
2. **Create the indicator subfolder** at `indicators/<group>/<indicator-name>/`
   using full descriptive names and language-appropriate casing.
3. **Create the required files:**
   - **Parameters** -- define a params struct with `BarComponent`,
     `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent` fields (all optional; zero = default
     in Go, `undefined` = default in TS).
     Document the defaults in field comments.
   - **Output enum** -- define a per-indicator output enum (e.g.,
     `SimpleMovingAverageOutput`) with named constants for each output.
   - **Implementation** -- embed `core.LineIndicator` (Go) or extend
     `LineIndicator` (TS). In the constructor:
     1. Resolve zero-value components → defaults for the component functions.
        In Go, check `if bc == 0 { bc = DefaultBarComponent }`.
        In TS, set the protected setters (`this.barComponent = params.barComponent`),
        which resolve `undefined` → default internally.
     2. Build the mnemonic using `ComponentTripleMnemonic` / `componentTripleMnemonic`
        with the **resolved** values (Go) or raw param values (TS — the function
        checks `!== undefined && !== Default*` itself).
     3. Initialize the `LineIndicator` with mnemonic, description, component
        functions, and the indicator's `Update` method.
   - **`Metadata()`** -- return a `core.Metadata` using the per-indicator output
     enum for `kind` (not a hardcoded `0`).
   - **Test file** -- include mnemonic sub-tests covering: all components zero,
     each component set individually, and combinations of two components.
4. **Register the indicator** in `core/indicator-type` (add a new enum variant).
5. **Follow the consistent depth rule** -- the indicator must be exactly two
   levels below `indicators/`.

## Design Decisions Log

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No underscore prefix on `core/`, `common/`, `custom/` | Leading underscores conflict with conventions in Python (`_` = private) and are non-idiomatic in Go. Dropping them gives true consistency across all five languages. The names are unambiguous enough on their own. |
| Full indicator names, not mnemonics | Short names (SMA, JMA) can be cryptic and may overlap. Full names are self-documenting. |
| `outputs/` instead of `entities/` in core | These types represent indicator *output shapes*, not general domain entities. `outputs` is more precise about their purpose. |
| `core/` instead of `types/` | `core` is broad enough to hold types, enums, base classes, and utilities without needing a rename later. `types` would be too narrow. |
| `common/` instead of `unknown/`, `classic/`, `standard/` | `common` is neutral -- doesn't imply age (`classic`), official status (`standard`), or sound dismissive (`unknown`). |
| `custom/` instead of `experimental/` | `custom` describes *origin* (you made it), not *maturity*. A custom indicator can be stable; an experimental one implies instability. If you need to track maturity, use metadata in code, not folder structure. |
| Go uses bare `Type` in packages; other languages use `IndicatorType`/`OutputType` | Go's package system provides scoping (`core.Type`), making prefixes redundant and stuttery (`core.IndicatorType`). Other languages import symbols directly, so self-describing names are necessary. |
| Per-indicator subfolders even in `common/` | Without subfolders, all indicator files in `common/` would land in one flat directory (and in Go, one package). This doesn't scale and is inconsistent with the author-folder structure. |
| Component enums start at `iota + 1` | Makes the zero value (`0`) explicitly "not set", which is used as the sentinel for "use default". This is idiomatic Go for optional enum fields in structs (zero value of `int` is `0`). |
| TypeScript enums start at `0`, components are `undefined` when not set | TS enums default to `0`, and optional params use `undefined` (not `0`) as the sentinel for "not set". The `componentTripleMnemonic` function checks `!== undefined && !== Default*` to decide whether to show a component. The `LineIndicator` setters use `=== undefined` (not truthiness) to decide when to apply the default, so explicitly passing a zero-valued enum (e.g., `BarComponent.Open`) works correctly. |
| Zero-value component = default, omitted from mnemonic | Keeps the common case clean (`sma(14)` instead of `sma(14, c, ba/2, p)`) while still allowing explicit overrides when needed. `ComponentTripleMnemonic` checks against `Default*` constants (not zero), so explicitly passing the default value also omits it — the same indicator always gets the same mnemonic. |
| Entity `Mnemonic()` method separate from `String()` | `String()` returns full words (`"close"`, `"mid"`) for JSON serialization and debugging. `Mnemonic()` returns short codes (`"c"`, `"ba/2"`) for compact display in chart labels. Separating them avoids overloading `String()` with a display concern. |
| Mnemonic sub-tests for every indicator | Mnemonic correctness matters for charting UIs. Each indicator must have sub-tests covering: all components zero, each component individually, and pairwise combinations. This prevents regressions when changing the format string or `ComponentTripleMnemonic`. |

## LineIndicator Design Pattern

A **line indicator** takes a single numeric input and produces a single scalar
output. Most indicators (SMA, EMA, JMA, ...) are line indicators. The
`LineIndicator` abstraction eliminates boilerplate by providing
`UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade` methods (and array variants) that delegate to the
indicator's core `Update(sample) → value` function.

### What Each Indicator Implements

Each concrete indicator is responsible for:

1. **`Update(sample float64) float64`** (Go) / **`update(sample: number): number`** (TS) --
   the core calculation logic.
2. **`Metadata()`** / **`metadata()`** -- returns indicator-level metadata with
   an explicit per-indicator output enum (not a hardcoded `kind: 0`).
3. **`IsPrimed()`** / **`isPrimed()`** -- whether the indicator has received
   enough data to produce meaningful output.

### What LineIndicator Provides (Eliminating Boilerplate)

The `LineIndicator` base provides:

- **`UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade`** -- all delegate to the indicator's `Update()`
  method via a function reference (Go) or abstract method (TS). The bar/quote/trade
  variants extract the relevant component value first.
- **Array update methods** -- `UpdateScalars/Bars/Quotes/Trades` (TS only;
  Go has equivalent free functions in `core/indicator.go`).
- **Storage of component functions** -- bar, quote, and trade component
  extraction functions are stored and used by the Update* methods.

### Go Implementation

In Go, `LineIndicator` is an **embedded struct**. The concrete indicator stores a
`core.LineIndicator` field and initializes it via `core.NewLineIndicator()`,
passing its own `Update` method as a function reference:

```go
type SimpleMovingAverage struct {
    mu sync.RWMutex
    core.LineIndicator   // embedded -- promotes UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade
    // ... indicator-specific fields
}

func NewSimpleMovingAverage(p *Params) (*SimpleMovingAverage, error) {
    sma := &SimpleMovingAverage{ /* ... */ }
    sma.LineIndicator = core.NewLineIndicator(
        mnemonic, desc, barFunc, quoteFunc, tradeFunc, sma.Update,
    )
    return sma, nil
}
```

The mutex lives on the concrete indicator (not on `LineIndicator`), and is
acquired inside `Update()`. The promoted `UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade` methods
call `updateFn` (which is the concrete indicator's `Update`), so the threading
model is preserved.

### TypeScript Implementation

In TypeScript, `LineIndicator` is an **abstract class**. The concrete indicator
extends it and implements `update()` and `metadata()`:

```typescript
export abstract class LineIndicator implements Indicator {
    protected mnemonic!: string;
    protected description!: string;
    protected primed!: boolean;

    public abstract metadata(): IndicatorMetadata;
    public abstract update(sample: number): number;
    // updateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade provided by base class
}
```

**Concrete indicator constructors must set the three component setters** to
initialize the component functions used by `updateBar()`, `updateQuote()`, and
`updateTrade()`:

```typescript
this.barComponent = params.barComponent;       // undefined → DefaultBarComponent
this.quoteComponent = params.quoteComponent;   // undefined → DefaultQuoteComponent
this.tradeComponent = params.tradeComponent;   // undefined → DefaultTradeComponent
```

The `LineIndicator` setters use `=== undefined` (not truthiness) to apply
defaults, so explicitly passing a zero-valued enum like `BarComponent.Open`
works correctly.

Each entity file also exports a `Default*` constant (`DefaultBarComponent`,
`DefaultQuoteComponent`, `DefaultTradeComponent`) for use in
`componentTripleMnemonic` and anywhere else defaults need to be checked.

### Per-Indicator Output Enums

Each indicator defines its own output enum describing what it produces:

```go
// Go
type SimpleMovingAverageOutput int
const SimpleMovingAverageValue SimpleMovingAverageOutput = iota
```

```typescript
// TypeScript
export enum SimpleMovingAverageOutput {
    SimpleMovingAverageValue = 0,
}
```

The `metadata()` method uses this enum for the `kind` field instead of a
hardcoded `0`, making the output semantics explicit and type-safe.

### Metadata Structure

The `Metadata` / `IndicatorMetadata` type includes:

| Field         | Description |
|---------------|-------------|
| `type`        | The indicator type enum variant (e.g., `SimpleMovingAverage`). |
| `mnemonic`    | A short name like `sma(5)` or `jma(7, -1)`. |
| `description` | A human-readable description like `Simple moving average sma(5)`. |
| `outputs`     | An array of per-output metadata (kind, type, mnemonic, description). |

The `mnemonic` and `description` live at the indicator level (on `Metadata`)
and are also duplicated per-output for convenience (each output carries its own
mnemonic/description).

### Component Triple Mnemonic

A line indicator can be fed bar, quote, or trade data. Each data type has a
**component** that selects which field to extract (e.g., close price from a bar,
mid-price from a quote). The `ComponentTripleMnemonic` utility (in `core/`)
generates the component suffix for an indicator's mnemonic. There are three
components (bar, quote, trade) -- hence "triple", not "pair".

#### Default Components

Each component type has a default constant defined in `entities/`:

| Component type   | Default constant         | Default value    | Mnemonic |
|------------------|--------------------------|------------------|----------|
| `BarComponent`   | `DefaultBarComponent`    | `BarClosePrice`  | `c`      |
| `QuoteComponent` | `DefaultQuoteComponent`  | `QuoteMidPrice`  | `ba/2`   |
| `TradeComponent` | `DefaultTradeComponent`  | `TradePrice`     | `p`      |

#### Zero-Value Convention

All component enums start at `iota + 1`, making the zero value `0` explicitly
"not set". In indicator params, **a zero-value component means "use the default
and omit from the mnemonic"**:

```go
// SimpleMovingAverageParams
type SimpleMovingAverageParams struct {
    Length         int
    BarComponent   entities.BarComponent   // zero → DefaultBarComponent, omitted from mnemonic
    QuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent // zero → DefaultQuoteComponent, omitted from mnemonic
    TradeComponent entities.TradeComponent // zero → DefaultTradeComponent, omitted from mnemonic
}
```

#### Constructor Resolution Logic

Each indicator constructor follows this pattern:

1. Read the component from params.
2. If zero, resolve to the default constant.
3. Get the component function using the resolved value.
4. Pass the **resolved** values to `ComponentTripleMnemonic` (which omits
   defaults automatically).

```go
// Resolve defaults for component functions.
bc := p.BarComponent
if bc == 0 {
    bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
}
// ... same for qc, tc

// Build mnemonic using resolved components — defaults are omitted by ComponentTripleMnemonic.
mnemonic := fmt.Sprintf("sma(%d%s)", length,
    core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc))
```

This means that `sma(14)` is the mnemonic whether the user left `BarComponent`
as zero or explicitly set it to `BarClosePrice` — both produce the same
indicator with the same mnemonic.

#### ComponentTripleMnemonic Function

The function compares each component against its `Default*` constant. If a
component equals its default, it is omitted. Non-default components are
appended as `", "` followed by the component's `Mnemonic()` output:

```go
func ComponentTripleMnemonic(
    bc entities.BarComponent, qc entities.QuoteComponent, tc entities.TradeComponent,
) string {
    var s string
    if bc != entities.DefaultBarComponent   { s += ", " + bc.Mnemonic() }
    if qc != entities.DefaultQuoteComponent { s += ", " + qc.Mnemonic() }
    if tc != entities.DefaultTradeComponent { s += ", " + tc.Mnemonic() }
    return s
}
```

The result is inserted into the indicator format string after the numeric
parameters: `sma(14%s)` → `sma(14)` or `sma(14, hl/2)`.

#### Mnemonic Examples

| Params                                                                | Mnemonic              |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| `{Length: 5}`                                                         | `sma(5)`              |
| `{Length: 5, BarComponent: BarMedianPrice}`                           | `sma(5, hl/2)`        |
| `{Length: 5, QuoteComponent: QuoteBidPrice}`                          | `sma(5, b)`           |
| `{Length: 5, TradeComponent: TradeVolume}`                            | `sma(5, v)`           |
| `{Length: 5, BarComponent: BarOpenPrice, QuoteComponent: QuoteBidPrice}` | `sma(5, o, b)`     |
| `{Length: 5, BarComponent: BarHighPrice, TradeComponent: TradeVolume}` | `sma(5, h, v)`       |
| `{Length: 7, Phase: 0}`                                               | `jma(7, 0)`           |
| `{Length: 7, Phase: 0, BarComponent: BarMedianPrice}`                 | `jma(7, 0, hl/2)`    |

The `description` is always the full name followed by the mnemonic:
`"Simple moving average sma(5, hl/2)"`.

#### Entity Mnemonic Methods

Each component enum has two display methods: `String()` (full word, used for
JSON serialization) and `Mnemonic()` (short code, used in indicator mnemonics).

**BarComponent mnemonics:**

| Enum value        | `String()`   | `Mnemonic()` |
|-------------------|-------------|---------------|
| `BarOpenPrice`    | `open`      | `o`           |
| `BarHighPrice`    | `high`      | `h`           |
| `BarLowPrice`     | `low`       | `l`           |
| `BarClosePrice`   | `close`     | `c`           |
| `BarVolume`       | `volume`    | `v`           |
| `BarMedianPrice`  | `median`    | `hl/2`        |
| `BarTypicalPrice` | `typical`   | `hlc/3`       |
| `BarWeightedPrice`| `weighted`  | `hlcc/4`      |
| `BarAveragePrice` | `average`   | `ohlc/4`      |

**QuoteComponent mnemonics:**

| Enum value             | `String()`     | `Mnemonic()`          |
|------------------------|----------------|-----------------------|
| `QuoteBidPrice`        | `bid`          | `b`                   |
| `QuoteAskPrice`        | `ask`          | `a`                   |
| `QuoteBidSize`         | `bidSize`      | `bs`                  |
| `QuoteAskSize`         | `askSize`      | `as`                  |
| `QuoteMidPrice`        | `mid`          | `ba/2`                |
| `QuoteWeightedPrice`   | `weighted`     | `(bbs+aas)/(bs+as)`   |
| `QuoteWeightedMidPrice`| `weightedMid`  | `(bas+abs)/(bs+as)`   |
| `QuoteSpreadBp`        | `spreadBp`     | `spread bp`           |

**TradeComponent mnemonics:**

| Enum value    | `String()`  | `Mnemonic()` |
|---------------|-------------|---------------|
| `TradePrice`  | `price`     | `p`           |
| `TradeVolume` | `volume`    | `v`           |

```
